zh.lifeSummary: 當Peter II of Sicily-Aragon於1305年7月24日出生在義大利西西利亞巴勒莫阿爾托方特時，他的父親Frederic de Sicily II   Roi de Sicile32歲，母親Éléonore d'Anjou Reine de Sicile21歲。他和Elisabeth Meinhardin von Kärnten und Tirol至少有3個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1342年8月15日在義大利西西利亞恩納卡拉希塔享年37歲，埋葬在義大利西西利亞巴勒莫巴勒莫三 馬爾科 埃萬傑利斯塔。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Peter II of Sicily-Aragon于1305年7月24日出生在意大利西西利亞巴勒莫阿爾托方特时，他的父亲Frederic de Sicily II   Roi de Sicile32岁，母亲Éléonore d'Anjou Reine de Sicile21岁。他和Elisabeth Meinhardin von Kärnten und Tirol至少有3个儿子和5个女儿。他去世于1342年8月15日在意大利西西利亞恩納卡拉希塔享年37岁，埋葬在意大利西西利亞巴勒莫巴勒莫三 馬爾科 埃萬傑利斯塔。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Peter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Peter II of Sicily-Aragon was born on 24 July 1305, in Altofonte, Palermo, Sicily, Italy, his father, Frederic de Sicily II   Roi de Sicile, was 32 and his mother, Éléonore d'Anjou Reine de Sicile, was 21. He had at least 3 sons and 5 daughters with Elisabeth Meinhardin von Kärnten und Tirol. He died on 15 August 1342, in Calascibetta, Enna, Sicily, Italy, at the age of 37, and was buried in San Marco Evangelista, Palermo, Palermo, Sicily, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Peter II of Sicily-Aragon est né le 24 juillet 1305, à Altofonte, Palermo, Sicile, Italie, son père, Frederic de Sicily II   Roi de Sicile, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Éléonore d'Anjou Reine de Sicile, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles avec Elisabeth Meinhardin von Kärnten und Tirol. Il est décédé le 15 août 1342, à Calascibetta, Enna, Sicile, Italie, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterré à San Marco Evangelista, Palermo, Palermo, Sicile, Italie.

Juli 1305 in Altofonte, Palermo, Sizilien, Italien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Frederic de Sicily II   Roi de Sicile 32 und seine Mutter Éléonore d'Anjou Reine de Sicile 21. Er hatte mit Elisabeth Meinhardin von Kärnten und Tirol mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 15. ko.lifeSummary: Peter II of Sicily-Aragon(이)가 1305년 7월 24일 이탈리아 시실리야 파일모 알토폰트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Frederic de Sicily II   Roi de Sicile(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Éléonore d'Anjou Reine de Sicile(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Elisabeth Meinhardin von Kärnten und Tirol(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1342년 8월 15일 이탈리아 시실리야 나 캘래스시베타에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 이탈리아 시실리야 파일모 파일모 산 마코 이반그리스타에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Peter II of Sicily-Aragon родился 24 июля 1305 г. в местности алтофонт, пеилирмо, сисиля, Италия, его отцу по имени Frederic de Sicily II   Roi de Sicile было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Éléonore d'Anjou Reine de Sicile, 21. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей от Elisabeth Meinhardin von Kärnten und Tirol. Он умер 15 августа 1342 г. в местности калассибетто, энна, сисиля, Италия в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности сан марко эвангелиста, пеилирмо, пеилирмо, сисиля, Италия.
